mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: langfuse_otel (python v3 SDK) callback does not nest calls properly
mission.description: [Bug]: langfuse_otel (python v3 SDK) callback does not nest calls properly. This issue affects 15+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### What happened?

Consider:

```
from langfuse import observe

import litellm
from litellm import completion
litellm.success_callback = ["langfuse_otel"]
litellm.failure_callback = ["langfuse_otel"]

(name="testing")
def f(a: int = 3) -> bool:

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: langfuse_otel (python v3 SDK) callback does not nest…
steps.description:
This PR implements a new Langfuse OpenTelemetry integration that allows LiteLLM to send observability data to Langfuse using the standardi
```yaml
from langfuse import observe

import litellm
from litellm import completion
litellm.success_callback = ["langfuse_otel"]
litellm.failure_callback = ["langfuse_otel"]

@observe(name="testing")
def f(a: int = 3) -> bool:
    completion(model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello, how are you?"}])
    return a > 2


f()
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: langfuse_otel (python v3 SDK) callback… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: This PR implements a new Langfuse OpenTelemetry integration that allows LiteLLM to send observability data to Langfuse using the standardi
codeSnippets:
from langfuse import observe

import litellm
from litellm import completion
litellm.success_callback = ["langfuse_otel"]
litellm.failure_callback = ["langfuse_otel"]

@observe(name="testing")
def f(a: int = 3) -> bool:
    completion(model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello, how are you?"}])
    return a > 2


f()

Overriding of current TracerProvider is not allowed

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
